cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking what upset Melanie during the National Championships. The options are A to D. She says she entered the National Championships, and even though she had the second fastest swim and the fastest run, she "came nowhere." Then she explains what happened: "I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running." From this, it looks like she made a mistake during the race, specifically in the cycling part. She followed someone incorrectly and ended up taking a wrong turn, which made her cycle an extra 20 km. This mistake led to her not performing well in the race, which obviously upset her. Looking at the options again: A) She was tricked by another competitor. But the passage says she was following a man, not necessarily that he tricked her. It sounds like she followed him by mistake, not that he intentionally tricked her. B) She felt she had let her teammates down. There's no mention of teammates here; the mistake was in her own race. C) She made a mistake during part of the race. This fits because she took a wrong turn during the cycling part, leading her to go 20 km too far.
